Algorithm: step-by-step

Source: vignettes/algorithm.Rmd
algorithm.Rmd

somalign aligns a query SOM to a fixed reference SOM using codebook-level KL-unbalanced entropic optimal transport. This vignette traces each stage from raw data to the final per-sample output columns.

Pipeline overview

{r overview, echo = FALSE, fig.height = 6, eval = requireNamespace("DiagrammeR", quietly = TRUE)} DiagrammeR::mermaid(" graph TD OD[Old data] --> NS[z-score normalise] ND[New data] --> RT[transform: ref mu sigma] NS --> RS[Reference SOM<br/>C_ref K x p] RT --> QS[Query SOM<br/>C_query M x p] RS --> S1[Stage 1: Direct Projection] QS --> S1 RS --> S2[Stage 2: OT on Codebooks] QS --> S2 S2 --> TP[Transport plan P<br/>M x K] TP --> S3[Stage 3: Correction Vectors] RS --> S3 S3 --> S4[Stage 4: Corrected Projection] TP --> S5[Stage 5: Label Transfer] S1 --> PR[old_som_unit<br/>old_som_label<br/>final_status] S4 --> AX[corrected_som_unit<br/>correction_norm<br/>transferred_label] S5 --> AX style PR fill:#d4edda,stroke:#28a745,color:#155724 style AX fill:#cce5ff,stroke:#004085,color:#004085 style S1 fill:#fff3cd,stroke:#856404 style S2 fill:#fff3cd,stroke:#856404 style S3 fill:#fff3cd,stroke:#856404 style S4 fill:#fff3cd,stroke:#856404 style S5 fill:#fff3cd,stroke:#856404 ")

Stage 0 — Training both SOMs

The reference SOM is trained on labelled old data after z-score normalisation (centre μ\mu and scale σ\sigma computed from old data). The query SOM is trained on new data transformed with the reference μ\mu and σ\sigma — not new-data z-scores — so both codebooks live in the same feature coordinate system.

Stage 1 — Direct projection (primary result)

Every new sample xsx_s is assigned to the nearest reference node: k*=arg⁡mink∥xs−Cref,k∥k^* = \arg\min_k \|x_s - C_{\text{ref},k}\|

No optimal transport is involved. This is the primary, conservative result.

Output: old_som_unit = k*, old_som_distance, outside_reference_distance, final_status, old_som_label, old_som_label_confidence.

Stage 2 — OT on codebooks

OT is solved once on the codebooks (MM query nodes × KK reference nodes), not on individual samples.

2a — Cost matrix

2b — OT objective

The transport plan PP minimises:

∑ijPijDij+ε⋅KL(P∥𝐚⊗𝐛)+ρq⋅KL(P𝟏∥𝐚)+ρr⋅KL(P⊤𝟏∥𝐛)\sum_{ij} P_{ij} D_{ij} + \varepsilon \cdot \mathrm{KL}(P \,\|\, \mathbf{a}\otimes\mathbf{b}) + \rho_q \cdot \mathrm{KL}(P\mathbf{1} \,\|\, \mathbf{a}) + \rho_r \cdot \mathrm{KL}(P^\top\!\mathbf{1} \,\|\, \mathbf{b})

where 𝐚\mathbf{a} and 𝐛\mathbf{b} are the query and reference node masses. The KL marginal penalties allow each side to discard unmatched mass: a novel query node can route only a small fraction of its mass to any reference node. Solved by alternating Sinkhorn scaling (pure-R or Python POT backend).

2c — Transport plan

match_fraction near 1 indicates a well-matched node; near 0 indicates a novel population with no good reference counterpart (label transfer will be rejected for such nodes).

Stage 3 — Correction vectors

For each query node ii the OT plan defines a weighted mean displacement toward its matched reference nodes:

Δi=∑jPij(Cref,j−Cquery,i)∑jPij\Delta_i = \frac{\displaystyle\sum_j P_{ij}\,(C_{\text{ref},j} - C_{\text{query},i})} {\displaystyle\sum_j P_{ij}}

∥Δi∥≈0\|\Delta_i\| \approx 0 means the query node already aligns with its reference counterpart. A large ∥Δi∥\|\Delta_i\| indicates a systematic displacement (batch effect or population shift).

Stage 4 — Corrected projection (auxiliary)

Every sample in query node ii is shifted by the same Δi\Delta_i and re-projected:

corrected_som_units=arg⁡mink∥(xs+Δi)−Cref,k∥\text{corrected\_som\_unit}_s = \arg\min_k \|(x_s + \Delta_i) - C_{\text{ref},k}\|

Both assignments are reported. old_som_unit (blue) is the primary result; corrected_som_unit (red) is auxiliary — use it for visualisation and triage.

Stage 5 — Label transfer (auxiliary)

For each query node ii the dominant reference node is identified from row P[i,⋅]P[i, \cdot], and its label is transferred if the acceptance thresholds are met.

Label transfer is rejected when match_fraction < 0.05 (novel node) or when the dominant reference node’s label purity (transferred_label_confidence) is below 0.60 (mixed node).

Output columns

Column Stage Purpose
old_som_unit 1 Primary node assignment
old_som_distance 1 Distance to that node
outside_reference_distance 1 Novelty flag
final_status 1 inside / outside / unknown
old_som_label 1 Primary label
old_som_label_confidence 1 Label purity at node
corrected_som_unit 4 OT-corrected assignment
correction_norm 3/4 Shift magnitude ∥Δi∥\|\Delta_i\|
transferred_label 5 OT-derived label
transferred_label_confidence 5 Purity at dominant ref node
transferred_label_accepted 5 Gate result (TRUE / FALSE)

OT runs on the M×KM \times K codebook, not on individual samples. Per-sample cost is O(n⋅K)O(n \cdot K) nearest-node search, chunked in somalign_fit().

On this page
